steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence announces "three types of muscle" and then lists smooth, cardiac, and skeletal. The blank must introduce that list with correct punctuation after a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'awkward phrasing before the list': the construction before the list is not a complete independent clause, so the punctuation is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — 'colon after "muscle tissue"': a colon correctly follows the independent clause and introduces the three-item list. ✓
  • Choice C — 'no punctuation': omitting punctuation runs the list into the clause without a clear separator. ✗
  • Choice D — 'period before the list': splits the sentence so the list becomes a fragment disconnected from the clause that introduces it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
